steps.title: Keep the future sequence
steps.content: The chapter description uses future tense: "we will focus." The next sentence describes another action that will happen next in the same chapter, so it should use simple future tense as well.

steps.title: Reject tense shifts
steps.content:
• Choice A: "Will have turned" is future perfect and implies completion before another future point, which is not signaled.
  • Choice B: "Turned" shifts to simple past.
  • Choice C: "Had turned" is past perfect and conflicts with the future framing.
The answer is D.
